trivia_bullets.text: "The hordechief's ground tactics were unsurpassed. If only he'd thought to watch the skies." [Scryfall]. No named speaker. The 'hordechief' framing implies a capable but terrestrially-focused adversary outmaneuvered by the aerial force — an in-world after-action observation.

The support keyword was introduced in Oath of the Gatewatch (2016), the second Zendikar-return set. After its debut it appeared in Battlebond (2018), Streets of New Capenna Commander (2022), Modern Horizons 2 (2021), and Modern Horizons 3 — used as a counter-distribution utility keyword across sets.
sources: Wizards: Oath of the Gatewatch Mechanics, magic.wizards.com, 2015-12-28
MTG Wiki: Support — via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Donato Giancola, one of the most decorated artists in science fiction and fantasy illustration. Giancola has produced 125+ MTG illustrations since 1996 (debut: Amber Prison, Mirage). He has won 23 Chesley Awards including a Lifetime Artistic Achievement Award (2002) and a World Fantasy Award; all MTG work is executed in traditional oils on panel.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Donato Giancola Land Panorama, December 2018

trivia_bullets.text: A 3/3 Flying Bird Soldier for {3}{W} with support 2 on entry. Common rarity makes it Pauper-legal. EDHREC rank 17,249 indicates modest Commander use in white counter-distribution shells; not a format staple.
sources: Scryfall API: legalities pauper: legal, edhrec_rank 17249
